metadata.title: EMA Reflection Paper on Regulatory Requirements for the Development of Medicinal Products for Acute Kidney Injury (AKI)
metadata.document_status: FINAL — adopted by CHMP 14 December 2023. Non-binding reflection paper.
metadata.notes: This is a final adopted reflection paper (non-binding) providing high-level guidance on developing medicinal products for prevention and treatment of AKI. Due to the limited regulatory experience in this field, detailed recommendations are limited. Rules extracted at MEDIUM/LOW severity since non-binding.

rules.rule_text: The proposed target indication for a medicinal product being developed should be clear with respect to prevention or treatment of AKI, as this distinction has important implications for the clinical development strategy.
rules.verbatim_quote: Differentiation between the prevention of AKI and the treatment of AKI is of relevance. The proposed target indication for the medicinal product being developed should be clear with respect to prevention or treatment of AKI, as this can have important implications for the clinical development strategy of the medicinal product.
rules.exceptions: None specified.

rules.rule_text: The KDIGO definition is the leading and globally accepted standard for diagnosing and staging AKI. Alternatives to this definition may be used if appropriately scientifically justified for a particular AKI setting.
rules.verbatim_quote: The Kidney Disease Improving Global Outcomes (KDIGO) definition is leading and globally accepted in diagnosing and staging of AKI. However, this general definition can have limitations in a specific development programme for a particular AKI setting, and alternatives to this definition may be used if appropriately scientifically justified.
rules.exceptions: Alternative definitions are acceptable if scientifically justified for the specific AKI setting.
cross_references: KDIGO Clinical Practice Guideline for AKI (2012)

rules.rule_text: Selection of patients for AKI studies should be appropriately justified with respect to AKI category, including those mentioned in section 4.1 (pre-renal, renal, and post-renal categories, including CSA-AKI and SA-AKI).
rules.verbatim_quote: Selection of patients for studies in the prevention and/or treatment of AKI should be appropriately justified with respect to AKI category, including those mentioned in section 4.1.
rules.exceptions: None specified.

rules.rule_text: Patients in AKI trials should generally be representative of the target population. Stratification according to important risk factors and/or the pathophysiological concept may be needed and should be justified.
rules.verbatim_quote: The patients included in the trials should generally be representative of the target population. Stratification according to important risk factors and/or the pathophysiological concept may be needed and should be justified.
rules.exceptions: None specified.

rules.rule_text: To ensure applicability of results to the European population, the standard of care should be sufficiently representative of European practice, independent of the dominating region(s) involved in study conduct.
rules.verbatim_quote: In order to ensure applicability of results to the European population, the standard of care should be sufficiently representative of European practice, independent of dominating region(s) involved in study conduct.
rules.exceptions: None specified.

rules.rule_text: Any strategies using biomarker(s) to identify patients for AKI trials should be appropriately justified. In the absence of a formally qualified biomarker, the use of a particular biomarker to identify the appropriate patient population should be justified based on understanding of pathophysiology and mechanism of action.
rules.verbatim_quote: However, in the absence of a formally qualified biomarker (or set of biomarkers) for the context of use of interest, the use of a particular biomarker to identify the appropriate patient population should be appropriately justified. Understanding of the pathophysiology and the mechanism of action of the medicinal product are important factors in justifying a particular strategy.
rules.exceptions: None specified.

rules.rule_text: If a specific patient phenotyping based on a biomarker set is selected for optimal effect of the medicinal product, this should be appropriately reflected in the indication. Any extrapolation to a broader population should be duly justified, and the clinical relevance of the biomarker as a companion diagnostic must be demonstrated.
rules.verbatim_quote: If, based on a biomarker set, a specific patient phenotyping is selected as needed for optimal effect of the medicinal product, this should be appropriately reflected in the indication. Any extrapolation to a broader population should be duly justified. Furthermore, specific requirements for the biomarker need to be demonstrated and justified, such as the clinical relevance of the use of the biomarker to select the appropriate patient phenotype for treatment with the medicinal product (e.g., as a companion diagnostic).
rules.exceptions: None specified.

rules.rule_text: Phase 2 studies in AKI should be designed to retrieve information on efficacy and safety effectively to subsequently optimally design the confirmatory phase. Critical elements include population, posology, selection of endpoints, timing of intervention, and follow-up time.
rules.verbatim_quote: Any phase 2 studies should be designed to retrieve information on efficacy and safety effectively, in order to be able to subsequently optimally design the confirmatory phase. Critical elements are e.g., the population to be included, posology, selection of endpoint(s) (measurements), timing of intervention and follow-up time.
rules.exceptions: None specified.

rules.rule_text: If the primary endpoint differs between phase 2 and phase 3 studies, it is highly recommended to include the planned phase 3 endpoint in the phase 2 trial as an additional endpoint.
rules.verbatim_quote: Furthermore, if the primary endpoint differs between phase 2 and phase 3 studies, it is highly recommended to include the planned phase 3 endpoint in phase 2 trial as an additional endpoint.
rules.exceptions: None specified.

rules.rule_text: A randomised double-blind placebo-controlled clinical study is likely to be the most preferable study design for AKI confirmatory trials, since no medicinal products are specifically licensed for treatment or prevention of AKI.
rules.verbatim_quote: Apart from standard clinical management including e.g., hemodynamic control, there are currently no medicinal products specifically licensed for treatment or prevention of AKI across different categories of AKI. Therefore, a randomised double-blind placebo-controlled clinical study is likely to be the most preferrable study design.
rules.exceptions: Patients should receive adequate background therapy in accordance with guideline recommendations; use of such therapy should be carefully documented.

rules.rule_text: Evidence based on a single pivotal confirmatory trial for AKI is not likely to be accepted unless further justified, given the very limited experience and absence of recently licensed products in this field.
rules.verbatim_quote: Since experience and positive clinical programmes are very limited and any (recently) licensed products are not available, evidence based on a single pivotal confirmatory trial is not likely to be accepted unless further justified.
rules.exceptions: May be accepted if further justified.

rules.rule_text: Endpoints for AKI trials should be carefully selected based on the mechanism of action, expected clinical effect, and possible consequences in relation to the indication being sought. Patient reported outcomes are not considered suitable as primary endpoints.
rules.verbatim_quote: Endpoints should be carefully selected based on the mechanism of action, expected clinical effect and possible consequences in relation to the indication being sought... Patient reported outcomes can be important and of clinical relevance but are not considered suitable as primary endpoint(s).
rules.exceptions: PROs may serve as secondary/exploratory endpoints.

rules.rule_text: The endogenous filtration marker creatinine is the most widely used and currently best available follow-up marker for renal function. The most accurate method of renal function estimation available should be used, with potential pitfalls (e.g., stability of marker over time, muscle mass alterations, volume status) appropriately addressed.
rules.verbatim_quote: The endogenous filtration marker creatinine is most widely used and currently the best available follow-up marker for renal function. The most accurate method of renal function estimation available should be used. In this context, any potential pitfalls using this endogenous filtration marker in relation to the method being used to estimate renal function should be appropriately addressed, e.g., stability of the marker over time, possible muscle mass alterations or volume status, etc.
rules.exceptions: Alternative markers may be used if appropriately justified.

rules.rule_text: Novel biomarkers for AKI are encouraged for evaluation but are currently not considered appropriate for key efficacy or safety evaluation. Risk scores are generally not acceptable to evaluate the key treatment effect in the confirmatory phase in absence of demonstrated robust correlations to clinically relevant treatment outcomes.
rules.verbatim_quote: Evaluation of novel biomarkers is encouraged but is currently not considered appropriate for key efficacy or safety evaluation. Also, risk scores are generally not acceptable to evaluate the key treatment effect in the confirmatory phase in absence of demonstrated robust correlations to clinically relevant treatment outcomes.
rules.exceptions: Novel biomarkers and risk scores can be helpful in phase 2 exploratory settings or as supportive endpoints in phase 3.

rules.rule_text: Major Adverse Kidney Events (MAKE) can be considered as a suitable composite endpoint for AKI trials. MAKE components can include a certain level of eGFR decline, receipt of new renal replacement therapy (RRT), and all-cause mortality. At least a 90-day follow-up period is recommended for efficacy evaluation.
rules.verbatim_quote: Major Adverse Kidney Events (MAKE) can be considered suitable as an endpoint for the evaluation of several different AKI categories... At least a 90-day follow-up period is recommended for efficacy evaluation as this is in the continuum of the renal pathophysiological process generally recognised as the time of translation from an acute to a more chronic phase.
rules.exceptions: Earlier time points for primary endpoint may be justified, but 3-month and preferably 6-month data should support the primary endpoint(s).

rules.rule_text: Components of the MAKE endpoint should demonstrate directional concordance and preferably comparable contribution to any possible beneficial treatment effect demonstrated by the composite, to avoid challenges in interpretation of efficacy results.
rules.verbatim_quote: Furthermore, components of the MAKE endpoints should demonstrate directional concordance and preferably comparable contribution to any possible beneficial treatment effect demonstrated by the composite, in order to avoid challenges in interpretation of the efficacy results.
rules.exceptions: None specified.

rules.rule_text: For sepsis-associated AKI, mortality is an important component of the primary endpoint and should be included as a secondary endpoint. A detrimental effect on mortality needs to be excluded at an adequate level of precision.
rules.verbatim_quote: Mortality is considered an important component of the primary endpoint and should be included as a secondary endpoint. In this context, a detrimental effect needs to be excluded at an adequate level of precision.
rules.exceptions: Any demonstration of superiority on mortality as primary endpoint would alleviate general safety requirements.

rules.rule_text: For cardiovascular surgery associated AKI (CSA-AKI) prevention, the occurrence of sustained GFR decline is the most clinically relevant endpoint. A composite of MAKE as a primary endpoint is considered reasonable.
rules.verbatim_quote: In a prevention of AKI setting, the occurrence of sustained GFR decline is the most clinically relevant endpoint to be evaluated... For the prevention of AKI in cardiovascular surgery, a composite of MAKE as a primary endpoint is considered reasonable.
rules.exceptions: None specified.

rules.rule_text: Trial planning, design, conduct, analysis and interpretation for AKI trials should be aligned with the estimand framework (ICH E9(R1)). All potential intercurrent events should be considered, and the treatment policy strategy is generally the primary efficacy assessment of interest.
rules.verbatim_quote: The scientific question(s) of interest, i.e., what the trial seeks to address and to quantify what is to be estimated (estimand), should be specified in all its attributes. The trial planning, design, conduct, analysis and interpretation should be aligned with the estimand framework... the outcome, regardless of the occurrence of intercurrent events (treatment policy strategy), is generally the primary efficacy assessment of interest.
rules.exceptions: Secondary estimand(s) of a hypothetical strategy could be proposed when considered of clinical or regulatory relevance.

rules.rule_text: The applicant is recommended to submit the clinical development plan to the competent authority within the framework of a scientific advice, to discuss and agree about the estimand and analysis plan.
rules.verbatim_quote: The applicant is recommended to submit the clinical development plan within the competent authority within the framework of a scientific advice, to discuss and agree about the estimand and analysis plan.
rules.exceptions: None specified.

rules.rule_text: At least safety data over six months period are expected for AKI medicinal products, although one-year exposure data is generally preferred. Any shorter follow-up would need appropriate justification.
rules.verbatim_quote: At least safety data over six months period are expected, although one-year exposure data is generally preferred. Any shorter follow-up would need an appropriate justification.
rules.exceptions: For SA-AKI, demonstration of superiority on mortality as primary endpoint would alleviate such general safety requirements. Shorter follow-up requires justification.

rules.rule_text: For the paediatric population, the KDIGO definition and staging system is most recent and preferred, though other AKI definitions may be justified depending on the clinical setting. Due to the wide range of normal creatinine levels in children of different sizes and ages, using serum creatinine to diagnose and stage AKI may be more challenging than in adults.
rules.source_section: Section 4.8 - Considerations for the paediatric population
rules.verbatim_quote: Several AKI definitions, including KDIGO, Paediatric Risk, Injury, Failure, Loss, End Stage Renal Disease (pRIFLE) and Acute Kidney Injury (AKIN), have been adapted to the paediatric population. The KDIGO definition and staging system is the most recent and the preferred one. However, depending on the clinical setting, other AKI definitions may be justified... Additionally, due to the wide range of normal creatinine levels in children of different sizes and age categories, the use of serum creatinine as a marker to diagnose and stage AKI and to determine treatment evidence may be more challenging than in adults.
rules.exceptions: Extrapolation from adult population may be foreseen per EMA/CHMP/13622/2022 (Structured guidance on extrapolation) if a sound extrapolation concept provides sufficient confidence.
